---
title: "Residual Cluade"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-15"
---

```{r}
# Select variables
pl <- pl_raw %>%
  select(
    any_of(c(
      "Date", "HomeTeam", "AwayTeam",
      "FTHG", "FTAG", "FTR",
      "HTHG", "HTAG",
      "HS", "AS", "HST", "AST",
      "HF", "AF", "HC", "AC",
      "HY", "AY", "HR", "AR",
      "B365H", "B365D", "B365A",
      "BWH",   "BWD",   "BWA",
      "IWH",   "IWD",   "IWA",
      "LBH",   "LBD",   "LBA",
      "PSH",   "PSD",   "PSA",
      "WHH",   "WHD",   "WHA",
      "VCH",   "VCD",   "VCA",
      "BbMxH", "BbMxD", "BbMxA",
      "BbAvH", "BbAvD", "BbAvA",
      "season"
    ))
  ) %>%
  mutate(Date = trimws(Date))

# Parse dates
pl$Date <- ifelse(
  nchar(pl$Date) == 8,
  as.character(as.Date(pl$Date, format = "%d/%m/%y")),
  as.character(as.Date(pl$Date, format = "%d/%m/%Y"))
)
pl$Date <- as.Date(pl$Date)

# Sort
pl <- pl %>%
  arrange(Date)

# Save — overwrites the old pl_clean.rds with all bookmaker columns included
saveRDS(pl, "pl_clean.rds")
```





```{r}
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BOOKMAKER ANALYSIS
# Option 1 — Multiple bookmaker comparison
# Option 2 — Favourite-longshot bias
# Option 3 — Residual correlation with match statistics
#
# Data source: pl_clean.rds
# Requires: log_loss and brier_score functions (defined below)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

library(tidyverse)
library(zoo)
library(purrr)

select <- dplyr::select
filter <- dplyr::filter

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 1: LOAD DATA
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

pl <- readRDS("pl_clean.rds")

team_map <- c(
  "Man City"      = "Manchester City",
  "Man United"    = "Manchester United",
  "Newcastle"     = "Newcastle United",
  "Nott'm Forest" = "Nottingham Forest",
  "West Brom"     = "West Bromwich Albion",
  "Wolves"        = "Wolverhampton Wanderers"
)

pl <- pl %>%
  mutate(
    HomeTeam = recode(HomeTeam, !!!team_map),
    AwayTeam = recode(AwayTeam, !!!team_map),
    Date     = as.Date(Date),
    home_win = as.integer(FTR == "H")
  )

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 2: EVALUATION FUNCTIONS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

log_loss <- function(actual, predicted) {
  p <- pmax(pmin(predicted, 1 - 1e-15), 1e-15)
  -mean(actual * log(p) + (1 - actual) * log(1 - p))
}

brier_score <- function(actual, predicted) {
  mean((actual - predicted)^2)
}

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 3: COMPUTE FAIR PROBABILITIES FOR ALL BOOKMAKERS
# Overround removed for each bookmaker separately
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

bookmakers <- list(
  B365 = c("B365H", "B365D", "B365A"),
  BW   = c("BWH",   "BWD",   "BWA"),
  IW   = c("IWH",   "IWD",   "IWA"),
  LB   = c("LBH",   "LBD",   "LBA"),
  PS   = c("PSH",   "PSD",   "PSA"),
  WH   = c("WHH",   "WHD",   "WHA"),
  VC   = c("VCH",   "VCD",   "VCA"),
  BbMx = c("BbMxH", "BbMxD", "BbMxA"),
  BbAv = c("BbAvH", "BbAvD", "BbAvA")
)

for (bm in names(bookmakers)) {
  cols <- bookmakers[[bm]]
  pl <- pl %>%
    mutate(
      impl_h    = 1 / .data[[cols[1]]],
      impl_d    = 1 / .data[[cols[2]]],
      impl_a    = 1 / .data[[cols[3]]],
      overround = impl_h + impl_d + impl_a,
      !!paste0("p_home_", bm) := impl_h / overround
    ) %>%
    select(-impl_h, -impl_d, -impl_a, -overround)
}

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 4: TRAIN / TEST SPLIT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

train_bm <- pl %>% filter(Date <  as.Date("2023-08-01"))
test_bm  <- pl %>% filter(Date >= as.Date("2023-08-01"))

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# OPTION 1 — MULTIPLE BOOKMAKER COMPARISON
# Fits a logistic model for each bookmaker using only their
# fair home win probability as predictor, evaluates on test set
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

bm_results <- map_dfr(names(bookmakers), function(bm) {
  col <- paste0("p_home_", bm)

  train_clean <- train_bm %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[col]]))
  test_clean  <- test_bm  %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[col]]))

  # Skip if not enough data in train or test
  if (nrow(train_clean) < 10 || nrow(test_clean) < 10) {
    cat("Skipping", bm, "— insufficient data\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  train_tmp <- train_clean %>% rename(p_bm = all_of(col))
  test_tmp  <- test_clean  %>% rename(p_bm = all_of(col))

  model <- glm(
    home_win ~ p_bm,
    data   = train_tmp,
    family = binomial(link = "logit")
  )

  preds <- predict(model, newdata = test_tmp, type = "response")

  tibble(
    bookmaker  = bm,
    log_loss   = round(log_loss(test_clean$home_win, preds),    4),
    brier      = round(brier_score(test_clean$home_win, preds), 4),
    avg_p_home = round(mean(test_clean[[col]], na.rm = TRUE),   4),
    avg_margin = round(mean(
      1 / test_clean[[bookmakers[[bm]][1]]] +
      1 / test_clean[[bookmakers[[bm]][2]]] +
      1 / test_clean[[bookmakers[[bm]][3]]] - 1,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ), 4)
  )
})

cat("=== Option 1: Bookmaker comparison ===\n")
print(bm_results)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# OPTION 2 — FAVOURITE-LONGSHOT BIAS
# Splits matches into probability buckets and compares implied
# probability against actual home win rate in each bucket
# bias = actual_rate - avg_implied
# Positive bias in high-p buckets = favourites underpriced
# Negative bias in low-p buckets  = longshots overpriced
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# 2a — Pinnacle only (sharpest market)
bias_pinnacle <- test_bm %>%
  filter(!is.na(p_home_PS)) %>%
  mutate(
    p_bucket = cut(p_home_PS,
                   breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1),
                   include.lowest = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  group_by(p_bucket) %>%
  summarise(
    n           = n(),
    avg_implied = round(mean(p_home_PS), 4),
    actual_rate = round(mean(home_win),  4),
    bias        = round(mean(home_win) - mean(p_home_PS), 4),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n=== Option 2a: Favourite-longshot bias — Pinnacle ===\n")
print(bias_pinnacle)

# 2b — Overall bias summary across all bookmakers
bias_summary <- map_dfr(names(bookmakers), function(bm) {
  col <- paste0("p_home_", bm)
  test_bm %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[col]])) %>%
    summarise(
      bookmaker    = bm,
      avg_implied  = round(mean(.data[[col]]),                  4),
      actual_rate  = round(mean(home_win),                      4),
      overall_bias = round(mean(home_win) - mean(.data[[col]]), 4)
    )
})

cat("\n=== Option 2b: Overall bias summary per bookmaker ===\n")
print(bias_summary)

# 2c — Bias by probability bucket across all bookmakers
bias_by_bm <- map_dfr(names(bookmakers), function(bm) {
  col <- paste0("p_home_", bm)
  test_bm %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[col]])) %>%
    mutate(
      p_bucket = cut(.data[[col]],
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1),
                     include.lowest = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    group_by(p_bucket) %>%
    summarise(
      bookmaker   = bm,
      n           = n(),
      avg_implied = round(mean(.data[[col]]), 4),
      actual_rate = round(mean(home_win),     4),
      bias        = round(mean(home_win) - mean(.data[[col]]), 4),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
})

cat("\n=== Option 2c: Bias by bucket across all bookmakers ===\n")
print(bias_by_bm)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# OPTION 3 — RESIDUAL CORRELATION WITH MATCH STATISTICS
# Pearson residuals from the bookmaker baseline correlated with
# rolling match stats to find what the bookmaker misses
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# 3a — Compute rolling 5-game averages of match stats
# Home team stats grouped by HomeTeam
home_stat_cols <- c("HS", "HST", "HC", "HF", "HY", "HR")
away_stat_cols <- c("AS", "AST", "AC", "AF", "AY", "AR")

for (col in home_stat_cols) {
  pl <- pl %>%
    group_by(HomeTeam) %>%
    arrange(Date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(
      !!paste0("home_", col, "_avg") :=
        lag(rollmean(.data[[col]], 5, fill = NA, align = "right"))
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
}

for (col in away_stat_cols) {
  pl <- pl %>%
    group_by(AwayTeam) %>%
    arrange(Date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(
      !!paste0("away_", col, "_avg") :=
        lag(rollmean(.data[[col]], 5, fill = NA, align = "right"))
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
}

# 3b — Re-split after adding rolling features
train_resid <- pl %>%
  filter(Date <  as.Date("2023-08-01"), !is.na(p_home_B365))
test_resid  <- pl %>%
  filter(Date >= as.Date("2023-08-01"), !is.na(p_home_B365))

# 3c — Fit bookmaker baseline and extract Pearson residuals
logit_book <- glm(
  home_win ~ p_home_B365,
  data   = train_resid,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)

train_resid <- train_resid %>%
  mutate(pearson_resid = residuals(logit_book, type = "pearson"))

# 3d — Correlate Pearson residuals with rolling match stats
rolling_cols <- c(
  paste0("home_", home_stat_cols, "_avg"),
  paste0("away_", away_stat_cols, "_avg")
)

cor_results <- map_dfr(rolling_cols, function(col) {
  ct <- cor.test(
    train_resid$pearson_resid,
    train_resid[[col]],
    use = "complete.obs"
  )
  tibble(
    variable    = col,
    correlation = round(ct$estimate, 4),
    p_value     = round(ct$p.value,  4),
    significant = ct$p.value < 0.05
  )
}) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(correlation)))

cat("\n=== Option 3: Residual correlations with match statistics ===\n")
print(cor_results)

# 3e — Fit extended model with significant variables
sig_vars <- cor_results %>%
  filter(significant == TRUE) %>%
  pull(variable)

cat("\nSignificant variables to add to model:\n")
print(sig_vars)

if (length(sig_vars) > 0) {

  formula_extended <- as.formula(
    paste("home_win ~ p_home_B365 +", paste(sig_vars, collapse = " + "))
  )

  train_extended <- train_resid %>%
    filter(complete.cases(across(all_of(sig_vars))))

  logit_extended <- glm(
    formula_extended,
    data   = train_extended,
    family = binomial(link = "logit")
  )

  summary(logit_extended)

  test_extended <- test_resid %>%
    filter(complete.cases(across(all_of(sig_vars))))

  preds_extended <- predict(logit_extended,
                            newdata = test_extended,
                            type    = "response")

  cat("\n=== Extended model vs bookmaker baseline ===\n")
  cat("Bookmaker log loss: ",
      round(log_loss(test_extended$home_win,
                     test_extended$p_home_B365), 4), "\n")
  cat("Extended log loss:  ",
      round(log_loss(test_extended$home_win,
                     preds_extended), 4), "\n")
  cat("Bookmaker Brier:    ",
      round(brier_score(test_extended$home_win,
                        test_extended$p_home_B365), 4), "\n")
  cat("Extended Brier:     ",
      round(brier_score(test_extended$home_win,
                        preds_extended), 4), "\n")

} else {
  cat("No significant variables found — bookmaker appears efficient\n")
}

# ── Option 3b: Residual correlation with xG features ───────────

match_xg <- readRDS("epl_match_xg.rds")

# Reshape to team level and compute rolling xG averages
model_df_bm <- bind_rows(
  match_xg %>% transmute(
    match_id, date = as.Date(date),
    team      = home_team,
    opponent  = away_team,
    home_away = "home",
    xG_openplay = home_xG_openplay,
    xG_corner   = home_xG_corner,
    xG_freekick = home_xG_freekick,
    xG_penalty  = home_xG_penalty,
    xG_setpiece = home_xG_setpiece
  ),
  match_xg %>% transmute(
    match_id, date = as.Date(date),
    team      = away_team,
    opponent  = home_team,
    home_away = "away",
    xG_openplay = away_xG_openplay,
    xG_corner   = away_xG_corner,
    xG_freekick = away_xG_freekick,
    xG_penalty  = away_xG_penalty,
    xG_setpiece = away_xG_setpiece
  )
) %>%
  arrange(team, date) %>%
  group_by(team) %>%
  mutate(
    roll_xG_openplay = lag(rollmean(xG_openplay, 5, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_corner   = lag(rollmean(xG_corner,   5, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_freekick = lag(rollmean(xG_freekick, 5, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_penalty  = lag(rollmean(xG_penalty,  5, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_setpiece = lag(rollmean(xG_setpiece, 5, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Collapse to match level
home_xg <- model_df_bm %>%
  filter(home_away == "home") %>%
  select(match_id, date,
         home_team        = team,
         away_team        = opponent,
         home_roll_xG_openplay = roll_xG_openplay,
         home_roll_xG_corner   = roll_xG_corner,
         home_roll_xG_freekick = roll_xG_freekick,
         home_roll_xG_penalty  = roll_xG_penalty,
         home_roll_xG_setpiece = roll_xG_setpiece)

away_xg <- model_df_bm %>%
  filter(home_away == "away") %>%
  select(match_id,
         away_roll_xG_openplay = roll_xG_openplay,
         away_roll_xG_corner   = roll_xG_corner,
         away_roll_xG_freekick = roll_xG_freekick,
         away_roll_xG_penalty  = roll_xG_penalty,
         away_roll_xG_setpiece = roll_xG_setpiece)

xg_match <- home_xg %>%
  left_join(away_xg, by = "match_id")

# Join xG features onto the training residuals data
train_resid_xg <- train_resid %>%
  left_join(
    xg_match,
    by = c("Date" = "date",
           "HomeTeam" = "home_team",
           "AwayTeam" = "away_team")
  )

# Correlate Pearson residuals with rolling xG features
xg_cols <- c(
  "home_roll_xG_openplay", "home_roll_xG_corner",
  "home_roll_xG_freekick", "home_roll_xG_penalty",
  "home_roll_xG_setpiece", "away_roll_xG_openplay",
  "away_roll_xG_corner",   "away_roll_xG_freekick",
  "away_roll_xG_penalty",  "away_roll_xG_setpiece"
)

cor_results_xg <- map_dfr(xg_cols, function(col) {
  ct <- cor.test(
    train_resid_xg$pearson_resid,
    train_resid_xg[[col]],
    use = "complete.obs"
  )
  tibble(
    variable    = col,
    correlation = round(ct$estimate, 4),
    p_value     = round(ct$p.value,  4),
    significant = ct$p.value < 0.05
  )
}) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(correlation)))

cat("\n=== Option 3b: Residual correlations — xG features ===\n")
print(cor_results_xg)
```
